title: Large case statements confuse output dependencies
body:
---


Author Name: **Charlie Brej**
Original Redmine Issue: 581 from https://www.veripool.org
Original Date: 2012-11-28
Original Assignee: Wilson Snyder (@wsnyder)

---

In a module that has many large case statements, the input to output dependencies seem to be corrupted. In the example, there is a completely unrelated assign from input to output that is affected by the cases in the module (although the same effect is present if it does use the result of the cases). Outside the module, the result of the modules is operated on, but although the results are updated, the operation refuses to execute and a static "vlTOPp->result = 0;" is generated in the cpp. Removing a few cases from the module fixes the issue, as does using casex and ? to reduce the case count.

Attached is a reproducer .v and .cpp that then saved to the same directory can be run with:
1. verilator --top-module reproducer -o veritest --cc reproducer.v --exe ../reproducer.cpp -Mdir reproducer
1. make -C reproducer/ -f Vreproducer.mk
1. reproducer/veritest
